---
editor_options:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(maps)
library(spdep)
library(MASS)
library(INLA)
library(ggplot2)
library(inlabru)
library(sf)


```

```{r}
# load data

load("Agrimonia_Dataset_v_3_0_0.Rdata")

whole_df <- AgrImOnIA_Dataset_v_3_0_0
# sample 50 000 rows to reduce size
set.seed=1
rows <- sample(1:dim(whole_df)[1], 50000, replace=F)
df <- whole_df[rows,]   # if we want a randomly subsetted dataset~1/6 of whole
df <- whole_df          # if we want the full dataset
```

```{r}

# create area_id column for spatial component (latent random effect)


# visualize the locations of our data
unique_locs <- unique(df[, c("Latitude", "Longitude")])

plot(unique_locs$Longitude, unique_locs$Latitude,
     pch = 19, col = "steelblue",
     xlab = "Longitude", ylab = "Latitude",
     main = "Monitoring Station Locations", xlim=c(5,15), ylim=c(40,50)) # the stations look pretty uniformly spread across the sampling area

map("world", region = "Italy", add = TRUE, col = "grey80")
points(unique_locs$Longitude, unique_locs$Latitude, pch = 19, col = "steelblue")

# create area_id column to use as spatial random effect
df$area_id <- as.integer(factor(paste(df$Latitude,df$Longitude,sep = "_")))


```

```{r}

# define a t_id temporal "season" to asses seasonal patterns (latent random effect)

# try using "month-year" as the seasonal t_id, so that we have a unique id for each month and year combination (RW1 might be good here instead of RW2 bc may be more sharp monthly changes)

df$t_id <- as.integer(factor(format(df$Time, "%Y-%m")))

# a month column to use for seasonal variation as a fixed effect
df$month <- as.factor(format(df$Time, "%m"))

```

**Jackson's preprocessing**

```{r}
# Main thing here is removing the 0 values (this will break a gamma likelihood)

summary(df$AQ_pm25) # lots of missing PM2.5 values
sum(df$AQ_pm25 == 0, na.rm = TRUE) # a few 0 values (also imply missing as 0 concentraiton is basically impossible)

# turn 0s into NaNs
df$AQ_pm25[df$AQ_pm25 == 0] <- NaN
# remove NaNs (and 0s) to analyse distribution of PM2.5
complete_case_df <- df[!is.na(df$AQ_pm25), ]
# note: can keep NaNs in modeling bc inla still works, but discludes NaNs in likelihood calculation but still outputs posterior predictions for them which may be useful

```

```{r}

statdomain <- fmesher::fm_nonconvex_hull(as.matrix(unique_locs[, 2:1]),
  convex = -0.05, concave = -0.1,
  resolution = c(100, 100))
plot(statdomain)

statmesh <- fmesher::fm_mesh_2d(boundary = statdomain,
  max.edge = c(0.1, 0.1), cutoff = 0.2)
plot(statmesh)
points(unique_locs[,2:1], col="red", pch=20)
```

**Model 2:** Start off by adding in a random effect for space

```{r}

Locations = data.frame(easting=df$Longitude, northing=df$Latitude) 

loc.spde = inla.spde2.pcmatern(mesh = statmesh,
           prior.range = c(1, 0.5), 
           prior.sigma = c(200, 0.1),constr=TRUE) 


df$geometry <- sf::st_as_sf(Locations, coords = c("easting", "northing"))$geometry

# making dummy variables for month - inlabru can't auto convert factors
df <- cbind(df, model.matrix(~month-1, df))

cmp <- AQ_pm25 ~ floc(geometry, model = loc.spde) + # random effects
       scale(Altitude) +  Intercept(1)     +        # fixed effects
       month02+month03+month04+month05+month06+month07+month08+month09+
       month10+month11+month12                 

mod2.bru <- bru(components=cmp, family = "Gamma",data=df)
summary(mod2.bru)


```

Visualising the space random effect

```{r}

meshlocs <- as.data.frame(statmesh$loc); names(meshlocs) <- c("lon","lat")
ggplot(meshlocs, aes(x = lon, y = lat, color = mod2.bru$summary.random$floc$mean)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_viridis_c() +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_minimal()

```

**Model 3:** Add in a random effect for time as well

```{r}
cmp <- AQ_pm25 ~ floc(geometry,model=loc.spde)+ftime(t_id, model="ar1")+ fmon()       
       scale(Altitude) + Intercept(1)+ # fixed effects
       


mod3.bru <- bru(components=cmp, family = "Gamma", data=df,
                options = list(control.compute = list(dic=T, cpo=T)
                               )
                )

summary(mod3.bru)
```

Visualising the random effects - now both spatial and temporal

\# temporal affect

plot(mod3.bru\$summary.random\$ftime\$mean, type="l")

```{r}
# spatial effect
meshlocs <- as.data.frame(statmesh$loc); names(meshlocs) <- c("lon","lat")
ggplot(meshlocs, aes(x = lon, y = lat, color = mod3.bru$summary.random$floc$mean)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_viridis_c() +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_minimal()

# temporal affect
plot(mod3.bru$summary.random$ftime$mean, type="l")

```

```{r}
# evaluate fits using WAIC

cat("M0 WAIC =",  m0$waic$waic, "\n")
cat("M1 WAIC =",  m1$waic$waic, "\n")
cat("M2 WAIC =",  m2$waic$waic, "\n")
cat("M3 WAIC =",  m3$waic$waic)



```
